title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
This Dhanteras Multiply Your Wealth with ULIP
Dhanteras – the combination of two words – ‘Dhan’ meaning money and ‘teras’ signifying the 13th lunar day on which the festival is celebrated.
Dhanteras is the celebration of money, of prosperity and of a financially rewarding year ahead. People venerate Goddess Lakshmi on this day and pray for her blessings, aka inflow of money. But does flow in without your efforts?
It doesn’t. You need to save and invest your savings into lucrative avenues to generate money. In fact, with the right investment, you can multiply your wealth over time. ULIPs can be one such investment that helps in wealth maximization.
What is a ULIP?
A ULIP is an investment-oriented life insurance policy that allocates your premium to capital market securities. The plan offers different types of funds and each fund invests in capital market instruments. With a ULIP, you can invest in the market and earn attractive returns.
Advantages of investing in a ULIP
There are various advantages that ULIPs can give. These include the following –
Different ULIPs for different needs
There are different types of ULIPs available in the market. Have a look –
Whole-life ULIPs that offer lifelong protection
Savings-oriented ULIPs to create a corpus for your goals
Child plans that help create a corpus for the child’s future
Pension ULIPs for retirement planning
Whatever your financial goal or need, you can find a ULIP to fulfil it. ULIPs, thus, prove relevant for all types of investors.
Exposure to market-linked returns
With ULIPs, you get market exposure. You can invest in a diversified portfolio of equity and/or debt instruments depending on your risk appetite. As you invest in market-linked securities, you get market-linked returns. If you invest in equity and the equity market rises, you can earn attractive returns on your portfolio. Similarly, debt instruments help you earn stable returns irrespective of market volatility.
You can use the ULIP calculator to estimate the returns that you can earn on your investments.
Creation of an inflation-proof corpus
As your investment is invested in market-linked assets, you don’t have to worry about inflation eating into your returns. Your returns are inflation-adjusted so that the corpus at the end of the tenure does not lose its real value and is sufficient to meet the inflated costs in the future.
Flexibility of managing your investments
ULIPs are flexible insurance plans that allow you to manage your investments as per your changing needs and strategies. Some of the flexible benefits that ULIPs provide include the following –
Partial withdrawals – if you need funds during the term of the policy, you can withdraw from the ULIP partially. These withdrawals are allowed after 5 years and are tax-free.
Premium redirection – You can choose to redirect your subsequent premium to another investment fund if you want. For instance, if you have invested in an equity fund and now want to invest in debt, you can choose the premium redirection option and start investing in debt.
Switching – Switching means changing the investment funds when your investment strategy changes. This helps you avoid investment risks and maximize returns. Moreover, switching your investment from one fund to another does not attract any tax since you stay invested in the plan.
Top-up premium – If you want to invest an additional premium in the ULIP to capitalize on the returns, you can do so through top-up premiums.
With these flexible benefits, you have complete control over your investments and also how they are managed.
Life insurance protection
ULIPs are life insurance plans. So, along with the investment returns that you can earn, you also get life insurance coverage. In the case of premature demise, the ULIP pays a guaranteed death benefit so that your family is compensated for the financial loss that they might suffer. Insurance protection is an added benefit that you get from the life insurance policy.
Tax benefits on investments and returns
If these benefits were not enough, ULIPs also have tax benefits that give you the most out of your savings. Have a look –
The premium paid for the plan is allowed as a deduction under Section 80C1 of the Income Tax Act, 1961. The limit is Rs.1.5 lakhs and the premium should be up to 10% of the sum assured.
The death benefit is always tax-free.
The amount that you switch does not attract any tax.
On maturity, the maturity benefit is tax-free if you bought the policy before 1st February 2021. If you buy after this date, the maturity benefit would be tax-free if the aggregate premium is up to Rs.2.5 lakhs. If the aggregate premium exceeds this limit, you have to pay capital gains tax on the returns from the policy2.
Why invest in Dhanteras?
Dhanteras celebrates wealth. What better day to invest than this?
People celebrate Dhanteras hoping the upcoming year to be financially rewarding. While you pray for a rewarding year, why not plan for it?
With ULIPs you can plan for financial rewards, both in the upcoming year and in subsequent years too. So, this Dhanteras, take a step to multiply your wealth. Use the ULIP calculator to find out how much you can earn. Invest in ULIPs for attractive returns, flexible investments and tax savings, all combined in one.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility

https://cleartax.in/s/80c-80-deductions

full_pdf_text:
Grow Your Wealth with ULIP
What is a ULIP?
A ULIP is an investment-oriented life insurance policy that allocates your premium to different market-linked funds, post deduction of applicable charges. The plan offers different types of funds to invest in and each fund invests in different asset classes like equity funds, debt funds etc. With a ULIP, you can invest in different funds as per your need and risk appetite and may earn market- linked returns.
Advantages of investing in a ULIP
There are various advantages that ULIPs can give. These include the following –
1. Different ULIPs for different needs
There are different types of ULIPs available in the market. Have a look –
o Whole-life ULIPs that offer lifelong protection o Savings-oriented ULIPs to create a corpus for your goals o Child plans that help create a corpus for the child’s financial future o Pension ULIPs for retirement planning Whatever your financial goal or need, you may find a ULIP to fulfil it. ULIPs, thus, may be considered for all types of investment goals. 2. Market-linked returns
With ULIPs, you get exposure to different market-linked funds. You can invest in a diversified portfolio of equity or debt funds, or combination of both, depending on your risk appetite. As you invest in market-linked funds, you may get market-linked returns. If you invest in equity funds and the equity market performs well, you may earn returns on your portfolio, depending on market conditions and other factors. Similarly, debt funds may help you earn stable returns as those have a fixed rate of return on the investment and therefore, are not exposed to market volatility You can use the ULIP calculator to estimate the market-linked returns that you may earn on your investments.
3. Flexibility of managing your investments
ULIPs are flexible insurance plans that allow you to manage your investments as per your changing needs and strategies. Some of the flexible benefits that ULIPs provide, subject to policy terms & conditions, include the following –
o Partial withdrawals – if you need funds during the term of the policy, you can
withdraw from the ULIP partially. These withdrawals are allowed after first 5 policy years. o Premium redirection – You can choose to redirect your subsequent premium to
another ulip fund if you want. For instance, if you have invested in an equity fund and now want to invest in debt, you can choose the premium redirection option and start investing future premiums in debt fund. o Switching – Switching means changing the ulip funds. This helps you avoid investment
risks and maximize returns. For instance, if the equity market is falling, you can switch
to a debt fund to protect your investments. Moreover, switching your investment from one fund to another does not attract any tax since you stay invested in the plan. o Top-up premium – If you want to invest an additional premium in the ULIP to
capitalize on the returns, you can do so through top-up premiums. With these flexibilities, you can manage your investments as per your requirements. 4. Life insurance protection
ULIPs are life insurance plans. So, along with the investment returns that you may earn, you also get life insurance coverage. In the case of unfortunate demise, the ULIP pays a guaranteed* death benefit so that the policyholder’s family is compensated for the financial loss that they might suffer. So, with ULIPs, you get insurance coverage as well as investment returns. 5. Tax benefits on investments and returns
If these benefits were not enough, ULIPs also have tax benefits . Have a look –
o The premium paid for the plan is allowed as a deduction under Section 80C1 of the
Income Tax Act, 1961 subject to provisions stated therein. The limit is Rs.1.5 lakhs and the premium should be up to 10% of the sum assured. o The death benefit is fully exempt from taxes o The amount that you switch does not attract any tax. o If ULIP policy is purchased before 1 Feb 2021 or if policy is purchased on or after 1 Feb
2021 with annual premium less than or equal to Rs. 2.50 lakhs, maturity benefit will be tax free in the hands of recipient subject to satisfaction of conditions mentioned in Section 10(10D) of Income Tax Act 1961. o If ULIP policy is issued on or after 1 Feb 2021 with aggregate annual premium more
than Rs. 2.50 lakhs, you have to pay capital gains tax on the returns from the policy2.
With ULIPs you can plan to build your wealth for your financial goals. Use the ULIP calculator to find out the expected corpus based on how much you invest. Invest in ULIPs for market-linked returns coupled with life cover, flexible investments and tax savings, all combined in one.
References:
1https://cleartax.in/s/80c-80-deductions
2https://www.livemint.com/money/personal-finance/heres-how-the-new-cbdt-circular-has- impacted-ulip-taxation-benefits-11642699866693.html
Disclaimer:
In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
*Conditions apply. The Guaranteed benefits are dependent on the policy terms, premium payment terms availed along with other variable factors. For details please refer to sales brochure (Also available on www.bajajallianzlife.com).
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility
